title: Dominium Player Desire Acceptance Map
repo_status: post-Ω/Ξ/Π and post-P-0..P-4 and post-0-0 and post-Λ-0 and post-Λ-1 and post-Λ-2 and post-Λ-3 and post-Λ-4
execution_context_note: Current execution context is post-Λ-4 with Λ-5 as the active prompt. Some earlier continuity docs still retain historical next-step references to Λ-0; this registry follows the explicit current checkpoint and does not treat stale next-step pointers as acceptance authority.

core_doctrine.summary: Dominium must be judged by the lawful desires its substrate can support, refuse, degrade, or defer without semantic drift.

actor_classes.summary: A player acting through embodied, local, diegetic, and law-governed pathways.
A player acting through abstract planning, reservation, delegation, or command pathways.
A non-player actor performing labor, service, logistics, or institutional roles under lawful authority.
An AI actor using lawful machine-readable surfaces and governed authority.
A guild, school, bureaucracy, factory, polity, or other organized actor acting through formal authority.
An observer, inspector, moderator, administrator, analyst, or remote operator acting through lawful observation and control surfaces.
A user or system acting through future adapters or new interface modes over the same substrate.
A machine or automation system operating within explicit protocol, authority, and verification limits.

desire_families.summary: Actors want to remain alive, viable, supplied, recoverable, and causally continuous.
desire_families.minimum_believable_support: Actors can detect survival risk, secure life-supporting inputs, preserve continuity of body and critical goods, and encounter explicit lawful deprivation instead of hidden invulnerability.

current_repo_planning_embodiment.notes: Authority, observation, maintenance, and civilization baselines ground the family, while detailed life-support and continuity systems remain later work.

desire_families.summary: Actors want to move through the world, inspect it, measure it, discover hidden structure, and turn uncertainty into grounded knowledge.
desire_families.minimum_believable_support: Exploration changes what can be known, not just what is rendered; actors can inspect, measure, and lawfully fail to know or reach things.

current_repo_planning_embodiment.notes: Observation, inspection, truth/perceived separation, and authority/epistemic docs already ground this family.

desire_families.summary: Actors want to live somewhere, eat, sleep, store, work, gather, travel locally, learn, belong, and maintain routine life as a first-class part of the world.
desire_families.minimum_believable_support: Homes, work, food, storage, routine travel, membership, and everyday obligations exist as lawful structures rather than decorative props.

current_repo_planning_embodiment.notes: Civilization, governance, and authority baselines support the family, while full ordinary-life substrate is still to be realized.

desire_families.summary: Actors want to make, adapt, repair, and combine tools, parts, and materials using lawful processes rather than recipe-only magic.
desire_families.minimum_believable_support: Fabrication depends on materials, tools, tolerances, and process law; parts and tools carry provenance and compatibility rather than existing as isolated recipe outputs.

current_repo_planning_embodiment.notes: Blueprint, material, and maintenance surfaces already give this family a strong substrate footing.

desire_families.summary: Actors want to invent machines, refine assemblies, define repeatable mechanisms, and move from local cleverness to engineering practice.
desire_families.minimum_believable_support: Engineering can produce novel assemblies, tested designs, and reusable machine families rather than only one-off craft outcomes.

current_repo_planning_embodiment.notes: Machine, blueprint, formalization, and authority surfaces already point toward this family.

desire_families.summary: Actors want systems to wear, fail, degrade, be diagnosed, serviced, repaired, and restored without hidden immortality.
desire_families.minimum_believable_support: Failures are inspectable, repair paths are explicit, degraded operation is lawful, and maintenance history matters.

current_repo_planning_embodiment.notes: Maintenance, decay, inspection, and operator doctrine already support this family strongly.

desire_families.summary: Actors want to delegate work to people, NPCs, institutions, and machines without collapsing agency, authority, or verification.
desire_families.minimum_believable_support: Delegated work preserves lawful authority, accountability, refusal, and inspection rather than granting invisible omnipotence.

current_repo_planning_embodiment.notes: Authority, AI/delegation, and planning/control docs already make this family materially present.

desire_families.summary: Actors want repeated production, specialized labor, throughput, quality control, and industrial coordination rather than perpetual artisanal one-offs.
desire_families.minimum_believable_support: Repeatable production depends on standards, infrastructure, labor, maintenance, and throughput constraints rather than menu magic.

current_repo_planning_embodiment.notes: Engineering and formalization roots exist, but detailed industrial systems are still later work.

desire_families.summary: Actors want to cultivate landscapes, manage populations, produce food, breed organisms, and shape ecology as lived substrate rather than background scenery.
desire_families.minimum_believable_support: Ecology and food systems affect survival, work, settlement, and trade across both local and large-scale horizons.

current_repo_planning_embodiment.notes: World and civilization baselines support the family, but detailed domain constitutions remain later work.

desire_families.summary: Actors want to move bodies, goods, information, and authority across space using routes, vehicles, docking, and dispatch structures.
desire_families.minimum_believable_support: Movement depends on lawful topology, occupancy, route state, infrastructure, and vehicle condition rather than free teleport semantics.

current_repo_planning_embodiment.notes: Route, network, transport, and authority materials already make this family concrete.

desire_families.summary: Actors want durable support systems such as power, water, signals, roads, pipes, grids, and service networks that make society work.
desire_families.minimum_believable_support: Utilities and networks have topology, maintenance burden, throughput limits, and public consequences instead of acting as invisible buffs.

current_repo_planning_embodiment.notes: Network, infrastructure, and authority docs already support this family, with detailed constitutions still pending.

desire_families.summary: Actors want to move goods, settle obligations, compare value, allocate labor, and run exchange systems across distance and scale.
desire_families.minimum_believable_support: Exchange depends on transport, contracts, accounting, standards, trust, and logistics rather than abstract currency-only convenience.

current_repo_planning_embodiment.notes: Planning doctrine strongly grounds the family, but concrete economic constitutions and systems remain later work.

desire_families.summary: Actors want places that grow from camps to towns to cities with civic function, neighborhoods, services, production, and political structure.
desire_families.minimum_believable_support: Settlements accumulate durable structure, governance, logistics, and everyday life rather than being static decoration clusters.

current_repo_planning_embodiment.notes: Civilization baselines already support this family with partial domain specificity.

desire_families.summary: Actors want jurisdiction, membership, procedure, obligation, enforcement, adjudication, and legitimate collective decision-making.
desire_families.minimum_believable_support: Law and governance exist as explicit institutions and procedures with inspection, refusal, jurisdiction, and lineage rather than hidden developer override.

current_repo_planning_embodiment.notes: Authority, governance, law, and audit materials already make this family core to the repo's doctrine.

desire_families.summary: Actors want to discover, test, teach, standardize, preserve, and propagate knowledge beyond local memory.
desire_families.minimum_believable_support: Knowledge can become explicit, testable, transferable, and institutionally propagated without losing provenance or being trapped in private intuition.

current_repo_planning_embodiment.notes: Λ-4, epistemics docs, and repo-wide concern for standards and lineage already strongly ground this family.

desire_families.summary: Actors want to defend, deter, threaten, contest territory, manage force, and survive organized conflict without magic combat abstractions severed from world law.
desire_families.minimum_believable_support: Conflict depends on logistics, authority, organization, material state, and continuity across local and strategic scales.

current_repo_planning_embodiment.notes: Law, authority, and civilization materials already touch this family, while detailed conflict work remains later.

desire_families.summary: Actors want expansion beyond one locale or planet, with lawful continuity across immense scale, infrastructure, institutions, and history.
desire_families.minimum_believable_support: Far-scale expansion remains one lawful world with recoverable lineage, infrastructure demands, and civilizational continuity rather than separate disconnected game layers.

current_repo_planning_embodiment.notes: Universe-scale and macro-capability materials already ground this family, though implementation depth remains future-facing.

desire_families.summary: Actors want to inspect truth lawfully, observe systems, troubleshoot, audit history, and operate mediated tools without becoming hidden authors of truth.
desire_families.minimum_believable_support: Observation, auditing, and operator tooling remain lawful projections over truth with explicit disclosure, refusal, and provenance.

current_repo_planning_embodiment.notes: Authority, observation, inspection, CLI, workspace, and spectator surfaces already make this family robustly present.

desire_families.summary: Actors want to extend the world, define alternate lawful experiences, and create new content without breaking the constitutional substrate.
desire_families.minimum_believable_support: Custom worlds and packs extend the lawful substrate through explicit contracts and refusal paths rather than arbitrary code-side branching.

current_repo_planning_embodiment.notes: Pack-driven integration and modding docs already give this family a strong constitutional footing.

desire_families.summary: Actors want delegated reasoning, machine orchestration, future tools, and new interfaces that still obey the same lawful reality.
desire_families.minimum_believable_support: AI and future interfaces use the same domain contracts, capability surfaces, and law envelopes as everyone else, with no hidden parallel ontology.

current_repo_planning_embodiment.notes: AI/autonomy, authority, observation, interface, and pack-driven surfaces already make this family materially present.

lambda_5_half: semantic ownership review can judge domains against concrete actor-purpose demand instead of abstract completeness
lambda_6: cross-domain bridge relevance can be tested against desire families that actually require shared substrate and scale transitions
sigma: governance, task, and agent-policy work can map actions and refusals to accepted actor-purpose families
phi: runtime priorities can be judged against believable support for ordinary life, engineering, institutions, logistics, and macro-micro continuity
upsilon: release and provenance work must preserve the acceptance floor for standards, operator audit, modding, and institutional lineage
zeta: late live-ops work must preserve continuity, auditability, and acceptance support rather than optimizing around a narrow combat or crafting loop
